title: Krozurbian

{{Quote|The first savorium-fed thookahs have already proven effective, as a Krozurbian War Council, fed on thookah-steaks, has turned away from two centuries of war and embraced peace.|[[PDA6]] describes the effect of savorium on the Krozurbians|<ref name="SOR4" />}}
The '''Krozurbians''' were a [[Color/Legends|green]]-skinned [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] native to the [[Krozurbia system/Legends|Krozurbia system]], near the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]]. At one point in [[Time/Legends|time]], they engaged in a [[Krozurbian war|war]] amongst themselves for two hundred years. That conflict was finally ended due to the intervention of the [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Zorneth]], who supplied the [[Krozurbian War Council]] [[Savorium herb|savorium]]-fed [[thookah]]s. Savorium was a drug that produced a pacifistic euphoria in its consumer, and the effect of steaks made from these thookahs on the Krozurbian War Council led them to embrace peace and end their war.

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|Does Zorneth have any thookahs with meat on their bones?|A Krozurbian merchant to [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]|<ref name="SOR1" />}}
The Krozurbians were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] with [[Color/Legends|green]] skin. The Krozurbian head was dominated by a large snout with a corrugated bridge. Their expressive [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] had two large, white, canine [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] on both the upper and lower jaws, betraying their [[Diet/Legends|carnivorous]] diet. Their snout curved back to two beady [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], which were white<ref name="SOR4" /> or red<ref name="SOR1" /> with black pupils. Their eyes were recessed under a heavy brow, and the Krozurbians also had a [[Bone/Legends|bony]] growth on each side of their snouts, partway toward the back of their heads. On the top of their heads, and running down their spine, were a series of blue scales. The Krozurbians were bipedal, and their [[Limb/Legends|hands]] had three fingers and an opposable thumb, while their feet had two large toes.<ref name="SOR4" />

==Society and culture==
Krozurbians wore various items of clothing, such as trousers, [[Shirt|tunics]] with piped shoulders, and [[Glove/Legends|gloves]]. For footwear, they wrapped cloth around their lower legs and feet. The Krozurbians also wore various accessories such as [[Bandolier/Legends|bandoliers]], [[Utility belt/Legends|utility belts]] with numerous pouches, and [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] [[Holster/Legends|holsters]].<ref name="SOR4" />

==History==
{{Quote|I laced herds of wooly thookahs with the herb so those who ate them would immediately become peaceful. I planned on spreading it throughout the galaxy, making all peoples peace-loving and harmless. What more could any Ithorian want?|Zorneth's journal|<ref name="The Essential Guide to Alien Species">[[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']]</ref>}}
[[File:Krozurbian_War_Council.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Krozurbian War Council embraces peace]]
The Krozurbians were native to the [[Krozurbia system/Legends|Krozurbia system]].<ref name="Planetnamia" /> This [[Star system/Legends|system]] was located in the [[Shadola sector/Legends|Shadola sector]],<ref name="TEAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] just north of the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]].<ref name="Outer Rim Extra">{{SWArchive|url=vault/books/atlas/news20090910/index.html|text=''Essential Atlas'' Extra: Outer Rim Sectors|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> Around [[206 BBY]],<ref>According to ''[[Star Wars Omnibus: Droids]]'', the story featured in [[Star Wars: Droids (1995)|''Star Wars Droids: Season of Revolt'']] is set five and a half [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], which puts it in [[6 BBY/Legends|6 BBY]]. Between the [[Droids (1995) 5|first]] and [[Droids (1995) 8|fourth]] issue, 200 years of Krozurbian [[Krozurbian war|war]] were brought to an end, placing the war's start in or around [[206 BBY]].</ref> [[Krozurbian war|internecine warfare]] broke out among the Krozurbians.<ref name="SOR4" />

That state of conflict endured for two hundred years, until the [[Krozurbian War Council]] consumed [[thookah-steak]]s made from [[thookah]]s raised by the [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Zorneth]]. Zorneth had worked with the [[Botanist/Legends|botanist]] [[Klorr Vilia]] when the latter discovered the [[savorium herb]], a drug that produced in its consumer a pacifistic rapture. After Vilia inadvertently succumbed to the drug's effect, Zorneth continued researching the [[Plant/Legends|herb]]. The Ithorian fed savorium to thookahs; as a result, consumption of the animals' meat produced the same euphoric effect as consumption of the herb. Zorneth's first herd of savorium-fed thookahs<ref name="SOR4" /> were sold at market to a [[Unidentified Krozurbian merchant|Krozurbian buyer]] at a loss, ostensibly in order to leave market quickly.<ref name="SOR1" /> The meat from these thookahs eventually became thookah-steaks that were fed to the Krozurbian War Council, and ultimately had a profound effect on the species: the War Council embraced peace, ending their intractable war.<ref name="SOR4" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Krozurbians were created by [[Jan Strnad]] for his [[Star Wars comic books|comic book series]] [[Star Wars: Droids (1995)|''Star Wars Droids: Season of Revolt'']], published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] in [[1995]]. They were illustrated by [[Bill Hughes]]. An unnamed merchant appeared in [[Droids (1995) 5|issue 1]],<ref name="SOR1" /> before the species' name and history were fleshed out in a flashback panel in [[Droids (1995) 8|issue 4]].<ref name="SOR4" /> Their home system was later included and placed in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>
